items.title: Augment X with a bias column
items.avoid: Fitting ŷ = Xw with no intercept when y has a natural baseline away from zero.
items.prefer: Append a column of ones or use library intercept support so b is learned explicitly.
items.why: The intercept absorbs global offset; without it, weights compensate incorrectly.

items.title: Standardize features for gradient descent
items.avoid: Running SGD on raw mixed-scale features (income in thousands, age in decades) with one global learning rate.
items.prefer: Standardize or normalize columns before iterative training; normal equation is scale-invariant for the fit itself.
items.why: Ill-conditioned X slows and destabilizes gradient steps even when the minimum is unchanged.
items.example: StandardScaler on X before manual GD loop; coef_ interpretability restored after inverse transform if needed.

items.title: Check rank before raw matrix inverse
items.avoid: w = inv(X.T @ X) @ X.T @ y on wide or duplicate-feature matrices.
items.prefer: np.linalg.lstsq, sklearn, or QR/SVD-based solvers; investigate collinearity if coefficients explode.
items.why: Numerical stability matters more than memorizing the inverse formula.
items.example: np.linalg.lstsq(X, y, rcond=None)[0] instead of inv(X.T @ X) @ X.T @ y.

items.title: Inspect residuals, not only MSE
items.avoid: Shipping the model when average squared error looks good but residuals fan out with ŷ.
items.prefer: Plot y − ŷ vs ŷ and vs each feature; consider transforms or nonlinear models if patterns appear.
items.why: OLS assumes errors are not systematically structured; patterned residuals mean the line is wrong.
items.example: U-shaped residual plot vs ŷ → try x² feature or switch model class.

items.title: Match evaluation to business units
items.avoid: Reporting only abstract MSE when stakeholders think in dollars or °C.
items.prefer: Report RMSE or MAE in target units on validation data alongside R².
items.why: RMSE = √MSE restores the scale of y for interpretable error bars.
items.example: Test RMSE = $12,400 on house prices is actionable; MSE = 153,760,000 is harder to read.
